context: Tuesday June 9 pre-market — one day before the May CPI print, the single binary that frames the entire week. The weekend relief bounce has FADED: BTC printed a 2026 cycle low $59,100 on Jun 6, retested ~$64K, then rolled back to $62.5K (-1.6% 24h / -10.1% on the week). Losing the $64K shelf triggered ~$1.1B in 24h liquidations. Fear & Greed sits at 10 (EXTREME FEAR) — deeper than Jun-7's 12 and Mon's 8 territory — with the whole basket red again on 24h. Live tape: BTC $62,561 (-1.57%), XRP $1.15 (-0.42%, relatively firm), SUI $0.746 (-1.58%), HBAR $0.0801 (-2.38%, weakest major today). Two NEW macro drivers since Jun 7: (1) CPI TOMORROW (Wed Jun 10) — 'a floor or a trapdoor'; (2) Kevin Warsh sworn in as Fed Chair this week and struck a HAWKISH tone, so markets are now pricing the possibility of 2026 rate HIKES rather than the cuts previously anticipated — a structural crypto headwind. Coinbase Premium Gap -$77 signals US institutional bid drying up; BTC ETF outflows ran $4.33B over 13 consecutive days through Jun 3 (one of the longest streaks on record). Counter-tell: Strategy (MicroStrategy) NET-added 1,550 BTC for $101M in early June (holdings 845,256 BTC) — the much-reported 32-BTC 'first-ever sale' was only ~$2.5M to cover preferred dividends, NOT capitulation. Regulatory tailwind intact but quiet: XRP's CLARITY bill is on the Senate Legislative Calendar (General Orders No. 423), eligible for a floor vote pending scheduling + a Banking/Ag text merge; spot XRP ETFs at $1.43B cumulative are diverging from BTC outflows. SUI's May outages are RESOLVED (Jun 1 upgrade + post-mortem) with a new WaterX tokenized-SpaceX RWA tailwind. Trade plan today: DO NOT pre-position size into CPI — wait for the print and the reaction candle. $60K is THE line for BTC; $59,100 the cycle floor; $64K the rejection shelf. Hold $60K post-CPI → relief into $64K; lose it → $57K/$55K. FOMC Jun 16-17 with a hawkish new Chair is the follow-on apex.

tickers.thesis: BTC is the macro driver going into a binary CPI print TOMORROW (Wed Jun 10). The Sunday relief bounce faded: BTC tagged the 2026 low $59,100 on Jun 6, rebounded to retest $64K, and has rolled back to $62,561 (-1.57% 24h, -10.08% 7d). Three things stack bearish into the data: (1) Kevin Warsh was sworn in as Fed Chair this week and struck a hawkish tone — markets are now pricing the possibility of 2026 rate HIKES, not cuts; (2) the Coinbase Premium Gap sits at -$77, US institutional bid drying up; (3) spot-BTC ETFs bled $4.33B over 13 consecutive days through Jun 3, one of the longest streaks on record. Counter-tell: Strategy NET-ADDED 1,550 BTC ($101M) in early June — the 32-BTC 'first sale' was just preferred-dividend cover, not capitulation. $60K is THE line: hold it and a hot-or-inline CPI can squeeze back to $64K; a daily close below reopens $57K then the $55K zone. Bias NEUTRAL with a bearish tilt — do NOT pre-size into CPI; let the print set direction.

tickers.thesis: XRP is the relative-strength name: down only -8.36% on the week vs the -10/-11% rest of the basket, and nearly flat on 24h (-0.42%) while BTC bleeds. The structural bid is the CLARITY Act + ETF divergence. CLARITY cleared Senate Banking May 14, was reported out of committee Jun 1, and now sits on the Senate Legislative Calendar (General Orders, Calendar No. 423) — formally eligible for a full-Senate floor vote. The remaining gates: leadership has to SCHEDULE the vote and merge the Banking text with the Senate Agriculture version first, so timing is the variable (some framing now points to a summer window, not imminent). Meanwhile spot XRP ETFs hold ~$1.43B cumulative inflows (May was a record $131.94M month) — money flowing IN while BTC ETFs bleed out, the cleanest relative-strength tell in the basket. Bias NEUTRAL/HIGH: range-trade $1.07-$1.27, $1.18 is the 24h cap. A scheduled floor-vote headline is the binary that re-rates the name toward the $1.55-$1.80 zone.

tickers.thesis: SUI's structural-reliability overhang is REPAIRED. The May 28-30 outages (3 halts / >15h downtime from the v1.72 gas-charging + randomness-state bugs) were fixed by the Jun 1 major upgrade — which shipped timestamp-based epochs, a Move code autoformatter, and improved GraphQL queries — and the Sui Foundation published a full post-mortem (no funds lost, no reverted transactions). With the overhang lifted, there's a fresh tailwind: WaterX sold tokenized SpaceX shares on Sui in under an hour, a clean RWA-demand signal. Price hasn't followed yet — SUI is -11.18% on the week and -1.58% on 24h with the macro tape. Bias moves to NEUTRAL (the structural SHORT thesis is gone): range-trade $0.733-$0.90. Reclaim $0.90 → momentum long; lose the $0.733 Tuesday low → $0.65 opens. Re-add SHORT only on a fourth outage.

insights.title: Sentiment flat: F&G 10 (Extreme Fear), Δ +2 vs yesterday
insights.body: Crypto Fear & Greed Index printed 10 (Extreme Fear) at 11:51Z — +2 vs yesterday's reading. F&G under 30 historically aligns with bounce-watch zones; under 20 with capitulation. Combined with the recent price action and live headlines (see LIVE FEED), the regime is risk-off until a sweep-and-reclaim candle prints. No new longs until F&G stops falling AND BTC reclaims its 24h high.

insights.title: Cross-exchange divergence on XRP: 0.42%
insights.body: Multi-source price triangulation (CoinGecko + Binance + Bybit) shows XRP divergence of 0.42% at 11:51Z. Divergence >0.5% across major venues is a thin-liquidity / arb-window signal. At 0.42% it's still in normal range but worth monitoring — widening = exit signal for sized trades on XRP.

insights.title: CPI Tomorrow Is THE Binary — Don't Pre-Position Into the Print
insights.body: May CPI drops Wed Jun 10 8:30 AM ET and it is the single event that frames the week — 'a floor or a trapdoor.' Two things have changed the backdrop since Jun 7: Kevin Warsh was sworn in as Fed Chair this week and struck a HAWKISH tone (markets now price the possibility of 2026 rate HIKES, not cuts), and the weekend relief bounce FADED with BTC rolling back to $62.5K after a $64K rejection. A hot core print hardens the hike narrative → BTC tests $60K, then the $59,100 cycle low, then $57K. A cool print → relief rally into the $64K rejection shelf. Trade plan: ZERO momentum size before the print. Let the reaction candle resolve, then trade the reclaim/rejection of $60K. FOMC Jun 16-17 with the dot-plot under a hawkish new Chair is the follow-on apex.

insights.title: Coinbase Premium -$77 + 13-Day ETF Outflow Streak = US Institutional Bid Drying Up
insights.body: Two aligned bearish tells under the surface: the Coinbase Premium Gap is -$77 (US spot trading at a discount to global = US institutions are NET SELLERS, not buyers), and BTC spot ETFs bled $4.33B over 13 CONSECUTIVE days through Jun 3 — one of the longest outflow streaks on record. Together with the Warsh-hawkish macro tightening, this explains why the weekend bounce had no follow-through. The counter-tell worth respecting: Strategy (MicroStrategy) NET-added 1,550 BTC for $101M in early June at ~$65,300 (holdings now 845,256 BTC). The widely-reported 'first-ever sale' was only 32 BTC (~$2.5M) to cover preferred dividends — NOT capitulation, and the Jun-7 'symbolic capitulation' read was wrong. So: institutional FLOW is risk-off, but the largest corporate holder is still accumulating.

insights.title: XRP Is the Relative-Strength Name — ETF Flows Diverge + CLARITY on Senate Calendar No. 423
insights.body: While BTC ETFs bled $4.33B/13 days, spot XRP ETFs sit at $1.43B cumulative (May was a record month at $131.94M of inflows) — a clean relative-strength divergence, and it shows in the tape: XRP -0.42% 24h vs the basket's -1.5% to -2.4%. Legislatively, the CLARITY bill cleared Senate Banking (May 14, reported out Jun 1) and now sits on the Senate Legislative Calendar, General Orders No. 423 — eligible for a floor vote, though leadership still must schedule it and merge the Banking text with the Senate Ag version. Range $1.07-$1.27; $1.18 is the 24h cap. This is a NEUTRAL/HIGH name: no momentum entry into CPI, but the structural bid (ETF + CLARITY) makes the OTE pullback to $1.073-$1.10 the cleaner long-side R:R if the basket holds.

insights.title: SUI's Structural-Short Thesis Is Gone — Outages Repaired, RWA Tailwind Added
insights.body: The reason SUI carried a structural SHORT bias in late May — the cluster of network outages — is RESOLVED. The Jun 1 upgrade shipped timestamp-based epochs, a Move autoformatter, and GraphQL fixes, and the Foundation published a post-mortem. New tailwind: WaterX sold tokenized SpaceX shares on Sui in under an hour (real RWA demand, not vaporware). So SUI is now NEUTRAL/MED, range-traded $0.733-$0.90; reclaim $0.90 → long, lose $0.733 → $0.65. Only re-add a SHORT on a 4th outage. SUI is -11.2% on the week (high-beta basket follower) so it'll move with the CPI reaction — but the idiosyncratic overhang that justified shorting it is lifted.

insights.title: HBAR Is the Laggard — Needs a >$0.11 Breakout to Change the Read
insights.body: HBAR is the weakest major on 24h (-2.38%) and -11.1% on the week, pinned at the low end of its $0.08-$0.11 range at $0.0801 — right on the $0.0800 support. The fundamental drivers are real but slow: the OKCoin Japan yen pair (HBAR/JPY) widens Asian retail access, and the Hedera Council + AI partnerships keep enterprise interest alive (forecasts cluster $0.09-$0.14). But nothing here is a near-term catalyst — HBAR is a range trade until it reclaims and holds above $0.11. NEUTRAL/MED. The OTE long into $0.0778-$0.0790 with stop $0.0755 is the only structured setup; otherwise it follows the basket through the CPI reaction.

insights.title: F&G 10 + Bounce Faded = No Durable Bottom Yet — CPI Is the Catalyst
insights.body: Fear & Greed at 10 (Extreme Fear) is deeper than Jun-7's 12, and crucially the weekend relief bounce that the Jun-7 report flagged has FADED — basket red again on 24h, BTC back to $62.5K after tagging $64K. Extreme Fear alone is not a buy signal; it's a buy signal only WITH a sweep-low + reclaim, and we don't have that yet. BTC printed a fresh 2026 cycle low $59,100 on Jun 6, so the structure is lower-lows. The catalyst that resolves this is tomorrow's CPI: a cool print + a $60K reclaim is the first credible bottoming attempt; a hot print sends the basket to retest $59,100 and below. Until then, Extreme Fear is a context flag, not a green light — keep risk small and let CPI pick the direction.
